name: Ceridian Dayforce HCM API
what_it_does: Ceridian Dayforce REST API for unified HCM platform with continuous payroll calculation. Enables AI agents to manage continuous payroll processing and real-time gross-to-net calculation, handle workforce scheduling and shift management automation, access time and attendance data collection and approval, retrieve talent management and performance data, manage recruiting and onboarding workflow automation, handle benefits administration and enrollment data, access compliance and regulatory reporting data, retrieve HR analytics and workforce intelligence dashboards, manage compensation and salary management workflows, and integrate Dayforce HCM data with ERP, finance, and workforce management systems.
use_cases: Automating workforce scheduling from shift management agents
Processing continuous payroll data from HR operations agents
Managing talent acquisition workflows from recruiting automation agents
Integrating HCM data with ERP from enterprise HR agents
not_for: Small businesses (under 100 employees) without Dayforce enterprise requirements
Simple payroll without advanced scheduling and workforce management needs
Contractor management without full-time W-2 employee HCM suite
best_when: A mid-market or enterprise using Ceridian Dayforce wants AI agents to automate workforce scheduling, continuous payroll calculation, time and attendance, talent workflows, and ERP data integration.
avoid_when: LEGAL+COMPLIANCE RISK: Automated workforce scheduling must comply with labor regulations (overtime, break requirements, predictive scheduling laws) which vary by jurisdiction. Payroll automation has IRS and state tax filing consequences. Talent management automation must respect EEOC compliance in hiring workflows.

auth.notes: Ceridian Dayforce uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. Client credentials with HR, payroll, and scheduling scopes. Developer portal with API documentation. Webhooks for HCM events. Dayforce Exchange for marketplace integrations. API access requires Dayforce customer relationship.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise subscription per employee
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise subscription; pricing per employee per module
pricing.notes: Minneapolis, Minnesota. NASDAQ: CDAY. Founded 1992 (formerly Ceridian). Dayforce platform launched 2012. $1.5B+ annual revenue. 6,000+ customers globally. Continuous payroll calculation (not batch) differentiator. Strong workforce management for hourly and shift-based workers. Competes with ADP, UKG, and Workday for HCM market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth application; bulk data export APIs available

known_agent_gotchas: LEGAL RISK: Automated scheduling must check local labor law (predictive scheduling, overtime, breaks) — violations carry penalties
Continuous payroll model — Dayforce calculates payroll continuously, not batch; understand data model differences vs traditional HCM
No public MCP server — OAuth2 REST API requiring commercial relationship
Dayforce Exchange approval — marketplace partner apps require Ceridian review process
API access restricted to Dayforce customers — no standalone developer API access
Multi-jurisdiction compliance — scheduling and payroll automation must handle US, CA, and EU jurisdiction differences
agent_readiness.security_notes: HCM and payroll data. SOC1, SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2. US/CA/EU/APAC. Employee PII, payroll, and scheduling data.

security_score_components.security_notes: HCM, payroll, and scheduling data. SOC1, SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2. Multi-region. Employee PII and workforce data.
